foundation.label: Foundation / Company / Localization
foundation.overview: Establishes company identity, localization context, fiscal baseline, and currencies on res.company. Every downstream domain depends on these foundational decisions being explicit and correct.

FND-FOUND-001.title: Company country configuration
FND-FOUND-001.what: Writes the primary operating country (country_id) onto res.company. This determines the legal and fiscal jurisdiction for the entire implementation.
FND-FOUND-001.why: Every localization package, tax rule, fiscal position, and compliance assumption derives from this setting. An incorrect country cascades errors into accounting, invoicing, and reporting.
confirm_first: What is the legal operating country for this company entity?
If multi-country operations exist, which country is the primary fiscal jurisdiction?
common_mistakes: Setting the country to the parent company's jurisdiction when the entity operates in a different country.
Changing the country after localization has been applied, which does not retroactively update dependent records.

FND-FOUND-002.title: Localization configuration
FND-FOUND-002.what: Applies the country-appropriate localization package (chart of accounts, tax baseline, fiscal positions) to res.company based on country_id.
FND-FOUND-002.why: The localization package determines the chart of accounts structure, default tax configuration, and fiscal position logic. Choosing the wrong localization means rebuilding accounting foundations later.
confirm_first: Has the correct country been confirmed in FND-FOUND-001?
Does the client need a specific chart of accounts variant within the localization (e.g., simplified vs. full)?
Is this an Enterprise or Community edition? Some localizations differ by edition.
common_mistakes: Applying localization before the country is confirmed, then needing to re-apply.
Assuming a generic localization is sufficient for a country with specific fiscal requirements.

FND-FOUND-003.title: Base currency acknowledgement
FND-FOUND-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the implementor has acknowledged the base currency setting on the company. No write is performed — FND-DREQ-002 handles the actual currency write.
FND-FOUND-003.why: The base currency cannot be changed after transactions are posted. Acknowledging this before proceeding prevents irreversible mistakes.
confirm_first: Is the base currency (set in FND-DREQ-002) correct for this entity's primary reporting currency?
Does the client understand that the base currency is effectively permanent once transactions post?
common_mistakes: Treating this as a formality without verifying the currency matches the entity's reporting requirements.

FND-FOUND-004.title: Fiscal country context acknowledgement
FND-FOUND-004.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the fiscal country context is understood and aligned with the localization selection.
FND-FOUND-004.why: Misalignment between the operating country and fiscal country creates downstream issues in tax computation, fiscal positions, and compliance reporting.
confirm_first: Does the fiscal country match the legal jurisdiction where this entity files taxes?
Are there any special economic zones or cross-border arrangements that affect fiscal treatment?
common_mistakes: Assuming the fiscal country is always the same as the operating country in cross-border setups.

FND-FOUND-005.title: Shared baseline settings acknowledgement
FND-FOUND-005.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that baseline company settings (language, timezone, operational defaults) are reviewed before downstream domains proceed.
FND-FOUND-005.why: Downstream domains inherit company-level settings. If baseline settings are wrong, every domain built on top of them will carry those errors.
confirm_first: Has the default language been set for the company?
Is the timezone correct for the primary operating location?
Are units of measure settings appropriate for the business (metric vs. imperial)?
common_mistakes: Leaving default settings from the localization package without reviewing them against business requirements.

FND-FOUND-006.title: Multi-company structure acknowledgement
FND-FOUND-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (BM-02=Yes). Informational confirmation that multi-company intent is acknowledged and will be reflected in downstream access and data design.
FND-FOUND-006.why: Multi-company mode fundamentally changes access rules, inter-company transactions, and data segregation. Enabling it later requires significant rework.
confirm_first: How many company entities will operate in this Odoo instance?
Will inter-company transactions be required?
Has the client confirmed the legal structure that maps to Odoo companies?
common_mistakes: Enabling multi-company without a clear inter-company transaction design.
Treating branches or departments as separate companies when they should be cost centers.

FND-DREQ-001.title: Fiscal year configuration
FND-DREQ-001.what: Configures the company fiscal year (start/end dates, reporting calendar) on res.company.
FND-DREQ-001.why: The fiscal year drives accounting period behavior, lock dates, and financial reporting boundaries. An incorrect fiscal year means reporting periods will not align with legal requirements.
confirm_first: What is the fiscal year start date for this entity?
Does the fiscal year follow a calendar year or a custom period?
Are there specific lock date requirements mandated by the client's auditors?
common_mistakes: Defaulting to calendar year without confirming with finance.
Setting the fiscal year after transactions have been posted in the wrong period.

FND-DREQ-002.title: Currency configuration
FND-DREQ-002.what: Sets the primary operating currency (currency_id) on res.company. This is the base currency for all accounting entries.
FND-DREQ-002.why: The base currency is functionally permanent after transactions post. Every journal entry, invoice, and financial report uses this as the reference currency.
confirm_first: What is the entity's primary reporting currency?
Does the client understand this cannot be changed after transactions are posted?
If multi-currency is needed later, is this the correct base for conversion?
common_mistakes: Setting USD when the entity reports in EUR, or vice versa.
Assuming the currency can be easily changed later — it cannot once transactions exist.

FND-DREQ-003.title: Multi-currency activation
FND-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (BM-04=Yes). Activates multi-currency accounting on res.company, enabling currency conversion, exchange rate management, and multi-currency journal entries.
FND-DREQ-003.why: Without multi-currency activation, the system cannot process transactions in foreign currencies. Enabling it later requires retroactive configuration that may conflict with existing entries.
confirm_first: Does the business transact in multiple currencies?
Has a currency exchange rate source been identified (manual or automatic)?
Has finance confirmed the exchange gain/loss account mapping?
common_mistakes: Activating multi-currency without setting up exchange rate providers.
Not configuring gain/loss accounts before processing foreign currency transactions.

FND-DREQ-004.title: Fiscal lock date policy
FND-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-04=Yes). Informational confirmation that the fiscal lock date policy has been reviewed and will be applied consistently.
FND-DREQ-004.why: Lock dates prevent retroactive changes to closed fiscal periods. Without a clear policy, users may modify posted entries in closed periods.
confirm_first: Who has authority to change lock dates?
Will lock dates be applied monthly, quarterly, or annually?
Does the client's audit process require specific lock date controls?
common_mistakes: Leaving lock dates unset, allowing anyone to modify historical entries.
Setting lock dates without communicating the policy to the finance team.

users_roles.label: Users / Roles / Security
users_roles.overview: Defines user accounts, role assignments, access rights groups, and approval responsibilities on res.users and res.groups. Every domain with restricted actions or approvals depends on this being correct.

USR-FOUND-001.title: User account provisioning foundation
USR-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the base user account configuration on res.users — language, timezone, and foundational user record settings.
USR-FOUND-001.why: User records are the identity layer for everything in Odoo. Incorrect base configuration (wrong language, missing fields) cascades into every domain that references the user.
confirm_first: Who are the initial users that need accounts?
What is the default language and timezone for user accounts?
Is there an existing user directory (LDAP/SSO) that will feed user creation?
common_mistakes: Creating users with admin access as a shortcut, then never restricting them.
Not setting the user language, leaving it mismatched with the company locale.

USR-FOUND-002.title: Access design boundaries acknowledgement
USR-FOUND-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that access design boundaries (admin vs. user, internal vs. portal) have been reviewed.
USR-FOUND-002.why: Access boundaries determine what data and actions each user can reach. Without explicit boundaries, the default is often too permissive or too restrictive.
confirm_first: Has the client identified which users are internal vs. portal?
Is there a separation of duties requirement (e.g., person who approves POs cannot also receive goods)?
Who is the designated system administrator?
common_mistakes: Giving all users full admin rights during setup and never revoking them.
Not distinguishing between portal users and internal users until go-live.
USR-FOUND-002.odoo_docs_hint: Settings > Users & Companies > Users > Access Rights tab

USR-DREQ-001.title: User role baseline assignment
USR-DREQ-001.what: Assigns baseline roles to provisioned users via res.users group assignments. Maps each user to their functional role (e.g., Sales User, Inventory User, Accounting Adviser).
USR-DREQ-001.why: Role assignments control what each user can see and do. Incorrect roles mean users either cannot perform their job or have access to data they should not see.
confirm_first: Has a role matrix been defined mapping job functions to Odoo access groups?
Are there users who need cross-domain access (e.g., sales + inventory)?
Has the client approved the initial role assignments?
common_mistakes: Assigning the broadest possible role to avoid access complaints during testing.
Not testing role assignments with actual workflows before go-live.
USR-DREQ-001.odoo_docs_hint: Settings > Users & Companies > Users > [User] > Access Rights

USR-DREQ-002.title: Access rights group configuration
USR-DREQ-002.what: Configures access rights groups on res.groups, defining the permission sets that roles reference. Controls model-level read/write/create/delete permissions.
USR-DREQ-002.why: Groups are the building blocks of Odoo access control. Misconfigured groups create security gaps or block legitimate operations.
confirm_first: Are the default Odoo groups sufficient or does the client need custom group modifications?
Have approval-chain groups been identified (e.g., purchase approval, sales approval)?
Is there a requirement for record-level security rules beyond group permissions?
common_mistakes: Modifying base groups without understanding implied group inheritance.
Creating overlapping custom groups that produce unpredictable combined permissions.

USR-DREQ-003.title: Privileged access review acknowledgement
USR-DREQ-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that privileged access (system admin, settings access, technical features) has been explicitly reviewed and scoped.
USR-DREQ-003.why: Privileged users can change system-wide settings, install modules, and bypass business rules. Uncontrolled privileged access is a security and compliance risk.
confirm_first: Who has Settings/Administration access and why?
Is Technical Features enabled for any non-developer users?
Has the client reviewed and accepted the privileged access list?
common_mistakes: Leaving multiple users with full admin access after initial setup.
Not documenting who has privileged access and why.
USR-DREQ-003.odoo_docs_hint: Settings > Users & Companies > Users > [User] > Administration dropdown

USR-DREQ-004.title: Technical administrator provisioning
USR-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-02=Yes). Provisions a dedicated technical administrator user account on res.users, separate from the business admin.
USR-DREQ-004.why: Separating technical administration from business administration provides audit clarity and reduces the risk of accidental configuration changes.
confirm_first: Who will serve as the technical administrator?
Is this person different from the business/functional administrator?
What specific technical permissions does this role require?
common_mistakes: Using a shared account for technical administration, losing audit traceability.

USR-DREQ-005.title: Multi-company access configuration
USR-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (BM-02=Yes). Configures multi-company group access on res.groups, enabling users to access records across company entities.
USR-DREQ-005.why: In multi-company setups, users must be explicitly granted access to each company. Without this, users cannot see records in other entities they need to work with.
confirm_first: Which users need access to which companies?
Are there users who should only see their own company's data?
Has inter-company access been reviewed against separation of duties requirements?
common_mistakes: Granting all users access to all companies without reviewing data sensitivity.
Forgetting to set the user's default company correctly.
USR-DREQ-005.odoo_docs_hint: Settings > Users & Companies > Users > [User] > Allowed Companies

USR-DREQ-006.title: Large-team access governance
USR-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (BM-05 > 50). Informational confirmation that access governance is appropriate for a large user base.
USR-DREQ-006.why: With more than 50 users, individual user management becomes impractical. Role-based governance and group templates become essential.
confirm_first: Is there a scalable approach to user provisioning (e.g., role templates)?
Has the client identified user groups by department or function?
Is there a process for onboarding/offboarding users?
common_mistakes: Managing users individually rather than through role templates at scale.

USR-DREQ-007.title: Discount authority access control
USR-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-02=Yes). Informational confirmation that discount authority has been mapped to specific user roles.
USR-DREQ-007.why: Uncontrolled discounting erodes margins. Mapping discount authority to roles ensures only authorized users can apply discounts beyond defined thresholds.
confirm_first: Which roles are authorized to apply discounts?
Are there discount limits by role or approval thresholds?
Has the sales policy for discounts been documented?
common_mistakes: Allowing all sales users to apply unlimited discounts.

USR-DREQ-008.title: Purchase approval authority mapping
USR-DREQ-008.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-02 != 'No approval'). Informational confirmation that purchase approval authority has been assigned to appropriate roles.
USR-DREQ-008.why: Purchase approvals are a key financial control. Without explicit authority mapping, either approvals are bypassed or the wrong people are approving expenditures.
confirm_first: Who can approve purchase orders?
Are there approval thresholds (e.g., orders above $X require manager approval)?
Has the approval chain been tested with realistic scenarios?
common_mistakes: Setting up approval rules but not assigning the approver role to any user.
USR-DREQ-008.odoo_docs_hint: Purchase > Configuration > Settings > Purchase Order Approval

USR-DREQ-009.title: Sales manager approval mapping
USR-DREQ-009.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-04='Manager approval'). Informational confirmation that manager-level sales approval authority has been assigned.
USR-DREQ-009.why: Manager approval for sales orders ensures oversight on deals that exceed normal parameters. Without the mapping, the approval workflow will not function.
confirm_first: Which users have the Sales Manager role?
What triggers the need for manager approval (discount threshold, order value)?
Has the approval workflow been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Enabling manager approval without assigning anyone the manager role, blocking all orders.

USR-DREQ-010.title: PLM/engineering access configuration
USR-DREQ-010.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-05=Yes). Informational confirmation that engineering and PLM access rights have been mapped to the appropriate users.
USR-DREQ-010.why: PLM access controls who can create, approve, and release engineering changes. Without proper mapping, engineering changes may be blocked or made by unauthorized users.
confirm_first: Who are the engineering users that need PLM access?
Are there separate roles for ECO creation vs. ECO approval?
Has the engineering team been briefed on the approval workflow?
common_mistakes: Giving all manufacturing users full PLM access when only engineers need it.
USR-DREQ-010.odoo_docs_hint: Settings > Users & Companies > Users > [User] > Manufacturing section

USR-DREQ-011.title: Analytic accounting access configuration
USR-DREQ-011.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-03=Yes). Informational confirmation that analytic accounting access has been properly configured for users who need cost center visibility.
USR-DREQ-011.why: Analytic accounting provides cost allocation and project costing capabilities. Users who need to tag entries or view analytic reports must have the appropriate access.
confirm_first: Which users need to create or view analytic accounts?
Is analytic accounting used for department cost centers, projects, or both?
Are there reporting requirements that depend on analytic data?
common_mistakes: Enabling analytic accounting without giving relevant users access to analytic menus.

master_data.label: Master Data
master_data.overview: Governs shared reference records (products, contacts, categories) on product.category, res.partner.category, and related models. Every downstream operational domain depends on master data structure being defined and consistent.

MAS-FOUND-001.title: Core master-data ownership defined
MAS-FOUND-001.what: Confirms that product category structure and ownership rules have been reviewed. Target: product.category. No governed write — confirm route only.
MAS-FOUND-001.why: Product categories drive accounting mapping, reporting grouping, and operational routing. Without clear ownership, categories proliferate without governance.
confirm_first: Who owns the product category taxonomy?
Has the existing category structure been reviewed for accuracy?
Are there business rules for when new categories can be created?
common_mistakes: Letting every department create categories without a naming convention.
Not reviewing the localization-installed default categories before adding custom ones.

MAS-FOUND-002.title: Shared classification structure defined
MAS-FOUND-002.what: Confirms that partner categories, product templates, and classification structures are reviewed and understood. Covers product.category, res.partner.category, and product.template.
MAS-FOUND-002.why: Classification structures affect how products appear in eCommerce, how partners are filtered in reporting, and how inventory is organized.
confirm_first: Has the partner category taxonomy been reviewed (customer types, vendor types)?
Are product templates structured for the business's actual product lines?
Is the UoM (unit of measure) configuration appropriate?
common_mistakes: Creating duplicate classification structures that overlap with existing ones.
Ignoring partner categories and losing segmentation capability for CRM and sales reporting.

MAS-DREQ-001.title: Product records readiness for downstream use
MAS-DREQ-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming product records (product.category structure) are ready for downstream domains to reference. No governed write.
MAS-DREQ-001.why: Sales, inventory, manufacturing, and purchasing all reference products. If product data is incomplete or miscategorized, every downstream domain inherits the problem.
confirm_first: Are the core products created with correct categories, types, and UoMs?
Have storable vs. consumable vs. service product types been correctly assigned?
Are product codes/references consistent with the client's naming convention?
common_mistakes: Importing products without setting the correct product type (storable vs. consumable).
Not assigning products to categories, making inventory valuation mapping impossible.

MAS-DREQ-002.title: Partner/contact category scaffolding
MAS-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming partner categories (res.partner.category) are structured for downstream CRM, sales, and purchase use. No governed write.
MAS-DREQ-002.why: Partner categories enable segmentation for marketing, reporting, and pricing rules. Without them, the client loses the ability to filter and analyze by customer/vendor type.
confirm_first: Has the client defined their customer and vendor segmentation categories?
Are partner categories aligned with CRM pipeline and sales team structure?
Will partner categories be used for automated pricing rules?
common_mistakes: Skipping partner categories entirely, then needing them for reports after go-live.

MAS-DREQ-003.title: Customer records readiness for downstream use
MAS-DREQ-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming customer records are structured for sales, invoicing, and eCommerce use. No governed write.
MAS-DREQ-003.why: Customer records with incorrect payment terms, fiscal positions, or missing addresses create invoicing failures and shipping errors in downstream domains.
confirm_first: Have key customer records been created with correct addresses, payment terms, and fiscal positions?
Are customer contacts linked to the correct parent company records?
Has the customer data import been validated against business requirements?
common_mistakes: Importing customers without payment terms, causing every invoice to use defaults.
Not linking contacts to parent companies, breaking the contact hierarchy.

MAS-DREQ-004.title: Data stewardship acknowledgement
MAS-DREQ-004.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that data stewardship rules (who can create/modify master data) have been reviewed and assigned.
MAS-DREQ-004.why: Without stewardship rules, master data quality degrades over time as multiple users create duplicate or inconsistent records.
confirm_first: Who is responsible for maintaining product data accuracy?
Who is responsible for maintaining contact/partner data?
Is there a process for requesting new master data records?
common_mistakes: Assuming data quality will self-govern without explicit ownership.

MAS-DREQ-005.title: Operations product category extension
MAS-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (OP-01=Yes). Informational confirmation that product categories are extended for inventory/operations use. Target model: product.category.
MAS-DREQ-005.why: Inventory and operations domains need specific category attributes (e.g., valuation method, costing method) set at the category level. Missing category configuration blocks inventory valuation.
confirm_first: Have product categories been configured with the correct inventory valuation method?
Are removal strategies (FIFO, LIFO) set at the category level where needed?
Has finance reviewed category-level accounting mappings?
common_mistakes: Leaving product category accounting fields blank, causing valuation entries to fail.
MAS-DREQ-005.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Account Properties

MAS-DREQ-006.title: Manufacturing product category configuration
MAS-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-01=Yes). Informational confirmation that product categories are configured for manufacturing use (BOM components, finished goods). Target model: product.category.
MAS-DREQ-006.why: Manufacturing depends on correct product categorization to drive BOM behavior, work order routing, and production cost allocation.
confirm_first: Are raw materials, semi-finished goods, and finished goods in distinct categories?
Has the costing method been set for manufacturing product categories?
Are BOM components correctly categorized as storable products?
common_mistakes: Mixing service products into manufacturing BOMs where storable products are required.

MAS-DREQ-007.title: Industry partner category scaffolding
MAS-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (inventory wizard completed, per wizard_captures.inventory). Configures industry-specific partner categories on res.partner.category for specialized segmentation.
MAS-DREQ-007.why: Industry-specific partner categories enable targeted pricing, reporting, and workflow automation that generic categories cannot support.
confirm_first: Has the client defined industry-specific partner segments?
Will these categories drive pricing rules or automated workflows?
Are the categories mutually exclusive or can partners belong to multiple categories?
common_mistakes: Creating too many granular categories that users will not consistently apply.

accounting.label: Accounting
accounting.overview: Governs fiscal setup, journals, taxes, chart of accounts, and financial control on account.journal, account.tax, and account.account. Finance policy checkpoints must be completed before valuation-sensitive operational checkpoints can proceed.

ACCT-FOUND-001.title: Accounting module activation foundation
ACCT-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the accounting module is activated and the journal baseline (account.journal) from localization is reviewed. No governed write.
ACCT-FOUND-001.why: Journals are the backbone of every accounting entry. The localization installs default journals that must be reviewed before any transactions are created.
confirm_first: Has the localization-installed journal list been reviewed?
Are all required journal types present (sales, purchase, bank, cash, miscellaneous)?
Do journal names match the client's accounting terminology?
common_mistakes: Starting to post entries before reviewing whether the default journals are appropriate.
Creating duplicate journals instead of renaming the localization defaults.

ACCT-FOUND-002.title: Chart of accounts configuration
ACCT-FOUND-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the chart of accounts (account.account, 124 records from localization on test236) has been reviewed. No governed write.
ACCT-FOUND-002.why: The chart of accounts determines how every financial transaction is categorized. Errors here affect every financial report and tax return.
confirm_first: Has the finance team reviewed the localization-installed chart of accounts?
Are there accounts that need to be added for the client's specific reporting needs?
Is the account code structure clear and consistent?
common_mistakes: Adding accounts without following the existing code structure.
Not mapping custom accounts to the correct account type, breaking financial reports.

ACCT-FOUND-003.title: Currency-accounting alignment
ACCT-FOUND-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that the accounting currency aligns with the company base currency set in foundation. Depends on FND-FOUND-003.
ACCT-FOUND-003.why: If accounting and company currencies are misaligned, every journal entry will have incorrect currency references, corrupting financial reporting.
confirm_first: Does the accounting currency match the company base currency from FND-DREQ-002?
If multi-currency is active, are the exchange rate accounts configured?
common_mistakes: Proceeding with accounting work before confirming currency alignment with foundation settings.

ACCT-DREQ-001.title: Journal configuration baseline
ACCT-DREQ-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming journal configuration (account.journal) is appropriate for the business. Reviews journal names, sequences, and default accounts.
ACCT-DREQ-001.why: Every transaction flows through a journal. Incorrect journal configuration causes entries to land in wrong accounts or fail validation.
confirm_first: Has each journal's default debit/credit account been reviewed?
Are bank journal names matching actual bank accounts?
Is the journal sequence numbering scheme acceptable to the client?
common_mistakes: Not linking bank journals to the correct bank account records.
ACCT-DREQ-001.odoo_docs_hint: Accounting > Configuration > Journals > [Journal] > Journal Entries tab

ACCT-DREQ-002.title: Tax configuration baseline
ACCT-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the tax baseline (account.tax, 16 records from localization on test236) is reviewed. No governed write.
ACCT-DREQ-002.why: Tax configuration drives every sales and purchase invoice. Incorrect tax rates, account mappings, or tax groups cause compliance failures.
confirm_first: Has the finance team reviewed the localization-installed tax records?
Are tax rates current for the applicable jurisdiction?
Are tax account mappings correct for both sales and purchase taxes?
common_mistakes: Assuming localization taxes are always current — tax rates change with legislation.
Not testing tax behavior on sample invoices before go-live.

ACCT-DREQ-003.title: Account code structure configuration
ACCT-DREQ-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the account code structure (account.account) is organized correctly for reporting and operational use. No governed write.
ACCT-DREQ-003.why: Account code structure determines report grouping, trial balance readability, and ease of financial analysis.
confirm_first: Is the account code numbering scheme clear and documented?
Are expense, revenue, asset, and liability accounts in the correct groups?
Does the structure support the client's management reporting needs?
common_mistakes: Adding accounts in random code ranges, making the chart of accounts unreadable.

ACCT-DREQ-004.title: Fiscal period configuration
ACCT-DREQ-004.what: Informational checkpoint confirming fiscal periods and reporting calendar are properly configured. No governed write.
ACCT-DREQ-004.why: Fiscal periods control when entries can be posted and how financial statements are grouped. Misconfigured periods cause entries to be posted in wrong periods.
confirm_first: Has the fiscal year been set correctly in FND-DREQ-001?
Are reporting periods monthly, quarterly, or custom?
Has the initial lock date policy been decided?
common_mistakes: Not setting an initial lock date, allowing entries to be posted in prior periods.

ACCT-DREQ-005.title: Multi-currency accounting activation
ACCT-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (BM-04=Yes). Activates multi-currency accounting journals on account.journal.
ACCT-DREQ-005.why: Multi-currency journals enable transactions in foreign currencies with automatic exchange rate differences. Without this, foreign currency entries cannot be properly recorded.
confirm_first: Which journals need multi-currency capability?
Has the exchange rate source been configured (ECB, manual)?
Are exchange gain/loss accounts mapped in the chart of accounts?
common_mistakes: Enabling multi-currency on all journals when only bank and sales journals need it.

ACCT-DREQ-006.title: AVCO/FIFO stock valuation accounts
ACCT-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-02=AVCO or FIFO). Configures stock valuation accounts on account.account for automated inventory valuation.
ACCT-DREQ-006.why: AVCO and FIFO valuation methods generate automatic accounting entries. Without correctly mapped accounts, stock valuation entries fail or land in wrong accounts.
confirm_first: Has finance confirmed the stock input, output, and valuation accounts?
Is the costing method decision (AVCO vs. FIFO) final?
Has the product category been configured to use automated inventory valuation?
common_mistakes: Setting up valuation accounts without aligning with the product category accounting properties.
Switching between AVCO and FIFO after initial inventory is loaded.
ACCT-DREQ-006.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Account Properties

ACCT-DREQ-007.title: Standard price variance accounts
ACCT-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-02=Standard Price). Configures price variance accounts on account.account for standard cost valuation.
ACCT-DREQ-007.why: Standard costing generates price variance entries when actual costs differ from standard costs. Without variance accounts, these entries have nowhere to go.
confirm_first: Has finance defined the price difference account for purchase price variances?
Is the standard cost update process documented?
Has the production variance account been mapped if manufacturing is in scope?
common_mistakes: Not configuring variance accounts, causing inventory receipts with price differences to fail.
ACCT-DREQ-007.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Account Properties > Price Difference Account

ACCT-REC-001.title: Analytic accounting configuration
ACCT-REC-001.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-05=Yes). Configures analytic accounting structure for cost center, project, or department-level financial tracking.
ACCT-REC-001.why: Analytic accounting enables cost allocation and profitability analysis. Without it, management reporting lacks the granularity needed for operational decisions.
confirm_first: What dimensions of analysis does the client need (department, project, product line)?
Has the analytic account plan been designed?
Will analytic tags or analytic plans be used?
common_mistakes: Creating an overly complex analytic structure that users will not consistently apply.

ACCT-REC-002.title: Full accounting reconciliation readiness
ACCT-REC-002.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-01=Full accounting). Informational confirmation that reconciliation processes are reviewed for full accounting scope.
ACCT-REC-002.why: Full accounting requires regular bank reconciliation, payment matching, and aged receivable/payable management. Without a process, the ledger drifts from reality.
confirm_first: Has the bank reconciliation process been documented?
Who is responsible for reconciliation and how often?
Are bank feeds or import files available for automated matching?
common_mistakes: Assuming reconciliation can be deferred until after go-live.

ACCT-GL-001.title: Accounting go-live readiness: account structure
ACCT-GL-001.what: Informational go-live checkpoint confirming the account structure is finalized and ready for operational use.
ACCT-GL-001.why: Changes to the account structure after go-live risk breaking existing entries and reports.
confirm_first: Is the chart of accounts finalized?
Have all required accounts been created and tested?
Has finance signed off on the account structure?
common_mistakes: Treating go-live readiness as automatic once accounts exist.

ACCT-GL-002.title: Accounting go-live readiness: closing procedures
ACCT-GL-002.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming that period closing procedures are documented and tested.
ACCT-GL-002.why: Without tested closing procedures, the first month-end close will be chaotic, with missing entries and unresolved items.
confirm_first: Has a month-end closing checklist been created?
Has a test close been performed in the staging environment?
Are lock date responsibilities assigned?
common_mistakes: Going live without ever running a simulated period close.

ACCT-GL-003.title: Accounting go-live readiness: opening balances
ACCT-GL-003.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming that opening balance entry procedures are planned and responsible parties are assigned.
ACCT-GL-003.why: Without correct opening balances, the entire general ledger starts from an incorrect position, invalidating all subsequent reporting.
confirm_first: Has the opening balance date been agreed with the client?
Who will prepare the opening balance journal entry?
Has the source of opening balances been identified (prior system, trial balance)?
common_mistakes: Posting opening balances without reconciling them against the prior system's closing balance.
ACCT-GL-003.odoo_docs_hint: Accounting > Journal Entries > Create (opening balance entry)

crm.label: CRM
crm.overview: Governs lead and opportunity pipeline structure, sales team assignments, and commercial qualification on crm.stage and crm.team. CRM design directly affects how opportunities flow into quotations and sales orders.

CRM-FOUND-001.title: CRM pipeline/stage activation foundation
CRM-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the CRM pipeline stage structure on crm.stage. Defines what stages opportunities pass through from initial lead to won/lost.
CRM-FOUND-001.why: Pipeline stages drive forecasting accuracy, activity reminders, and management reporting. A poorly designed pipeline makes CRM data unreliable.
confirm_first: Has the client defined their sales process stages?
How many pipeline stages are needed (avoid too many — 5-7 is typical)?
Does the stage structure reflect probability weighting for forecasting?
common_mistakes: Creating too many stages, causing sales reps to skip stages and break reporting.
Not setting probability percentages on stages, making forecasting meaningless.

CRM-FOUND-002.title: CRM team/user assignment foundation
CRM-FOUND-002.what: Establishes sales team structure on crm.team, mapping teams to users and defining team-level pipeline access.
CRM-FOUND-002.why: Sales teams control lead assignment, pipeline visibility, and team-level reporting. Without teams, all leads go to everyone or no one.
confirm_first: How many sales teams are needed?
Who are the team leaders?
Will leads be auto-assigned by rules or manually assigned?
common_mistakes: Creating only one team, losing the ability to segment pipeline reporting.
Not assigning team leaders, which breaks assignment rules.

CRM-DREQ-001.title: Pipeline stage configuration
CRM-DREQ-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming pipeline stage configuration (crm.stage) is aligned with the sales process. No governed write.
CRM-DREQ-001.why: Stage names, sequences, and probability settings must match the actual sales workflow or CRM data becomes unreliable.
confirm_first: Have the default stages been renamed to match the client's sales process?
Are stage probabilities set for accurate forecasting?
Is the win/lost stage configuration correct?
common_mistakes: Keeping default Odoo stage names that do not match the client's process terminology.

CRM-DREQ-002.title: Lead assignment rules
CRM-DREQ-002.what: Configures lead assignment rules on crm.team, defining how new leads are distributed to teams and salespeople.
CRM-DREQ-002.why: Without assignment rules, leads accumulate unassigned and are never followed up. This directly impacts revenue.
confirm_first: Should leads be assigned by geographic territory, product interest, or round-robin?
What happens to leads that match no assignment rule?
Has the assignment logic been tested with sample leads?
common_mistakes: Creating assignment rules that overlap, causing leads to be assigned to multiple teams.

CRM-DREQ-003.title: Activity/pipeline discipline
CRM-DREQ-003.what: Configures activity and pipeline discipline rules on crm.stage, ensuring opportunities have required follow-up activities at each stage.
CRM-DREQ-003.why: Without activity discipline, opportunities stagnate in stages without follow-up, and management cannot identify bottlenecks.
confirm_first: Are there mandatory activities at each pipeline stage?
What is the expected time in each stage before escalation?
Has the activity type list been customized for the business?
common_mistakes: Not setting rotting time on stages, so stale opportunities are never flagged.

CRM-DREQ-004.title: Team activity tracking
CRM-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-02=Yes). Configures team-level activity tracking on crm.team for teams that require activity monitoring.
CRM-DREQ-004.why: Team activity tracking provides management visibility into sales team productivity and follow-up compliance.
confirm_first: Which teams need activity tracking enabled?
What KPIs will be monitored at the team level?
Has the reporting dashboard been configured for team leads?
common_mistakes: Enabling tracking without defining what constitutes meaningful activity metrics.

CRM-REC-001.title: CRM reporting/team readiness
CRM-REC-001.what: Informational recommended checkpoint confirming CRM reporting structure and team configuration are ready for operational use.
CRM-REC-001.why: CRM reporting accuracy depends on correct stage, team, and activity configuration. Going live with misconfigured CRM produces misleading dashboards.
confirm_first: Has the CRM dashboard been reviewed with sales leadership?
Are pipeline and forecast reports producing expected results with test data?
Have sales team leads validated their team configuration?
common_mistakes: Assuming CRM reports will be accurate without verifying stage probabilities and team assignments.

sales.label: Sales
sales.overview: Governs quotation-to-order flow, pricing, and commercial execution policy on product.pricelist and related settings. Sales configuration drives how quotes are created, priced, and converted to orders.

SAL-FOUND-001.title: Sales master data readiness acknowledgement
SAL-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming that products and customer records are ready for sales use. No governed write.
SAL-FOUND-001.why: Sales cannot create accurate quotations without correct product records, prices, and customer data from master data.
confirm_first: Are the products that will appear on quotations created with correct sales prices?
Are customer records complete with correct addresses and payment terms?
Has the pricelist structure been decided?
common_mistakes: Starting to create quotations with incomplete product or customer records.

SAL-FOUND-002.title: Sales module configuration foundation
SAL-FOUND-002.what: Establishes sales module configuration on product.pricelist, defining the baseline pricelist and sales policy settings.
SAL-FOUND-002.why: The default pricelist determines pricing for all quotations unless overridden. Getting this wrong means every quote uses incorrect prices.
confirm_first: What is the default pricing strategy (fixed price, discount-based, formula)?
How many pricelists are needed (e.g., retail, wholesale)?
Has the default pricelist currency been confirmed?
common_mistakes: Creating pricelists without testing them on sample quotations.
Not setting the default pricelist on the sales order form.

SAL-DREQ-001.title: Quotation-to-order baseline / commercial policy
SAL-DREQ-001.what: Configures the quotation-to-order conversion policy on product.pricelist, including quotation validity, confirmation workflow, and order numbering.
SAL-DREQ-001.why: Commercial policy determines how quotes become binding orders. Without a clear policy, quotes may be confirmed without proper authorization.
confirm_first: What is the default quotation validity period?
Is a quotation confirmation email sent to the customer?
Does the client require a separate order number from the quotation number?
common_mistakes: Setting quotation validity too long, creating stale quotes with outdated pricing.

SAL-DREQ-002.title: Pricing baseline
SAL-DREQ-002.what: Configures pricing baseline rules on product.pricelist, defining how prices are computed and what discounts apply.
SAL-DREQ-002.why: Pricing rules affect every quotation line. Incorrect rules result in wrong prices that may only be caught after invoicing.
confirm_first: Are pricing rules set for all relevant customer segments?
Has the client provided a price list or pricing matrix?
Are volume discounts or tiered pricing needed?
common_mistakes: Configuring complex pricing rules without testing them against real-world scenarios.

SAL-DREQ-003.title: Pricelist discount rules
SAL-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-02=Yes). Informational confirmation that discount rules are configured and mapped to authorized users.
SAL-DREQ-003.why: Discount rules without user-level authorization controls allow unauthorized discounting.
confirm_first: Which users can apply manual discounts?
Are discount limits defined per role or pricelist?
Has the discount approval workflow been tested?
common_mistakes: Enabling discounts without setting limits, allowing 100% discounts.

SAL-DREQ-004.title: Quotation template configuration
SAL-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-03=Yes). Configures quotation templates for standardized sales proposals on product.pricelist.
SAL-DREQ-004.why: Quotation templates ensure consistent sales proposals and reduce manual errors in line item configuration.
confirm_first: What quotation templates does the client need?
Should templates include pre-defined product lines or optional products?
Will templates be used for online quotation signing?
common_mistakes: Creating templates that do not reflect actual product bundles or service offerings.

SAL-DREQ-005.title: Manager approval pricing
SAL-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-04='Manager approval'). Configures manager approval rules for sales orders.
SAL-DREQ-005.why: Manager approval ensures oversight on orders that exceed normal parameters, protecting margins and ensuring compliance with commercial policy.
confirm_first: What triggers the need for manager approval?
Who has the Sales Manager role to approve?
Has the approval workflow been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Setting approval triggers without assigning anyone the authority to approve.

SAL-DREQ-006.title: Invoicing policy linkage
SAL-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-06=Yes). Configures invoicing policy settings linking sales orders to invoice generation.
SAL-DREQ-006.why: Invoicing policy determines whether invoices are generated on order confirmation or on delivery. The wrong policy creates cash flow timing issues.
confirm_first: Should invoicing happen on ordered quantities or delivered quantities?
Does the policy differ by product type (goods vs. services)?
Has finance approved the invoicing policy?
common_mistakes: Invoicing on ordered quantities when the business ships partial orders.

SAL-DREQ-007.title: Dropship/delivery pricing
SAL-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-05=Yes). Configures delivery and dropship pricing rules on product.pricelist.
SAL-DREQ-007.why: Dropship and delivery pricing rules ensure shipping costs are correctly computed and passed through to customers.
confirm_first: Are delivery methods configured with correct pricing?
How should dropship costs be reflected on sales orders?
Are there free shipping thresholds?
common_mistakes: Not configuring delivery carriers, causing shipping lines to show zero cost.

SAL-GL-001.title: Sales go-live readiness
SAL-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming sales configuration is finalized and ready for production quotations.
SAL-GL-001.why: Going live with sales before confirming pricing, templates, and approval workflows risks incorrect quotes reaching customers.
confirm_first: Has the sales team tested the complete quote-to-order workflow?
Are all pricelists, templates, and approval rules finalized?
Has the sales team been trained on the new workflow?
common_mistakes: Going live with sales without running a parallel test with real customer scenarios.

purchase.label: Purchase
purchase.overview: Governs supplier procurement, purchasing control, and inbound commercial commitments on res.company (via res.config.settings). Purchase configuration controls RFQ flow, approval thresholds, and vendor terms.

PUR-FOUND-001.title: Purchase baseline acknowledgement
PUR-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming vendor/partner records are ready for purchase use. No governed write.
PUR-FOUND-001.why: Purchase orders require complete vendor records with correct payment terms, lead times, and currency settings.
confirm_first: Are key vendor records created with correct payment terms and lead times?
Are vendor pricelists configured if applicable?
Has the vendor approval process been documented?
common_mistakes: Creating purchase orders before vendor records are complete, causing incorrect billing.

PUR-DREQ-001.title: RFQ-to-purchase-order purchase policy settings
PUR-DREQ-001.what: Configures RFQ-to-PO conversion policy on res.company (via res.config.settings), including purchase order locking and receipt reminders.
PUR-DREQ-001.why: Purchase policy settings control how RFQs become binding purchase orders. Without configuration, orders may be confirmed without proper controls.
confirm_first: Should confirmed purchase orders be locked from editing?
Are receipt reminder emails needed?
What is the default RFQ validity period?
common_mistakes: Not locking confirmed POs, allowing post-confirmation edits that are not communicated to vendors.

PUR-DREQ-002.title: Vendor terms and pricing policy settings
PUR-DREQ-002.what: Configures vendor terms and pricing policy on res.company, establishing default vendor payment terms and pricing behavior.
PUR-DREQ-002.why: Vendor terms affect cash flow forecasting and vendor relationship management. Incorrect defaults create unnecessary manual corrections.
confirm_first: What are the standard vendor payment terms?
Are there vendor-specific pricing agreements that need to be configured?
Has the client provided a vendor master list with terms?
common_mistakes: Using a single default payment term for all vendors when different terms are negotiated.

PUR-DREQ-003.title: Purchase approval threshold settings
PUR-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-02=Threshold). Configures threshold-based purchase approval on res.company (po_double_validation_amount).
PUR-DREQ-003.why: Approval thresholds ensure that purchases above a defined amount receive management oversight before being confirmed.
confirm_first: What is the approval threshold amount?
Who are the designated approvers?
Has the threshold been reviewed against typical purchase order values?
common_mistakes: Setting the threshold too low, causing unnecessary approval bottlenecks.
Setting it too high, effectively removing the control.
PUR-DREQ-003.odoo_docs_hint: Purchase > Configuration > Settings > Purchase Order Approval

PUR-DREQ-004.title: Purchase approval all-orders setting
PUR-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-02=All orders). Configures all-order purchase approval on res.company (po_double_validation='always').
PUR-DREQ-004.why: When all purchase orders require approval, there must be designated approvers available to avoid blocking procurement.
confirm_first: Are there enough approvers to handle the volume of purchase orders?
Is there a backup approver for when the primary is unavailable?
Has the approval workflow been tested with realistic volume?
common_mistakes: Requiring approval on all orders without considering the approval bottleneck this creates.
PUR-DREQ-004.odoo_docs_hint: Purchase > Configuration > Settings > Purchase Order Approval

PUR-DREQ-005.title: Purchase-accounting linkage settings
PUR-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-03=Yes). Configures analytic accounting linkage for purchase orders on res.company.
PUR-DREQ-005.why: Purchase-accounting linkage enables cost allocation of purchases to analytic accounts (cost centers, projects).
confirm_first: Will purchase orders be tagged with analytic accounts?
Has the analytic account plan been configured in ACCT-REC-001?
Who is responsible for selecting the correct analytic account on POs?
common_mistakes: Enabling analytic tagging without training procurement staff on when to use it.

PUR-DREQ-006.title: Subcontracting purchase settings
PUR-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-04=Yes). Informational confirmation that subcontracting purchase settings are configured. Depends on MRP-DREQ-007.
PUR-DREQ-006.why: Subcontracting in Odoo links purchase orders to manufacturing. Misconfigured settings cause component tracking and cost allocation failures.
confirm_first: Has the subcontracting workflow been defined with manufacturing?
Are subcontractors set up as vendors with the correct subcontracting location?
Has component shipping to subcontractors been tested?
common_mistakes: Not configuring the subcontracting location, causing components to be consumed from the wrong warehouse.

PUR-DREQ-007.title: Dropship purchase settings
PUR-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-05=Yes). Informational confirmation that dropship routes and purchase settings are configured.
PUR-DREQ-007.why: Dropshipping sends goods directly from vendors to customers. Without correct route and purchase configuration, orders fail to create vendor deliveries.
confirm_first: Which products use the dropship route?
Are vendor lead times set for dropship products?
Has the dropship delivery flow been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Enabling dropship routes without configuring vendor delivery addresses.

PUR-GL-001.title: Purchase go-live readiness
PUR-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming purchase configuration is finalized and ready for production procurement.
PUR-GL-001.why: Going live with purchase before approval workflows and vendor terms are set creates uncontrolled procurement.
confirm_first: Has the procurement team tested the full RFQ-to-PO-to-receipt workflow?
Are all approval rules and vendor terms finalized?
Has the procurement team been trained?
common_mistakes: Going live without testing the approval workflow with actual approvers.

inventory.label: Inventory
inventory.overview: Governs warehouse structure, operation types, routes, and valuation on stock.warehouse and stock.picking.type. Inventory design affects every physical goods movement and links directly to accounting through valuation.

INV-FOUND-001.title: Warehouse structure foundation
INV-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the warehouse structure on stock.warehouse, defining warehouses, their short codes, and initial location hierarchy.
INV-FOUND-001.why: Warehouses are the top-level container for all inventory operations. Wrong warehouse design means every pick, pack, and ship operation uses incorrect locations.
confirm_first: How many physical warehouses does the business operate?
What are the warehouse names and short codes?
Will multi-step receiving or shipping be used?
common_mistakes: Creating multiple warehouses when the business has one physical location with multiple areas.
Not setting warehouse short codes, causing operation sequence confusion.

INV-FOUND-002.title: Warehouse operation baseline acknowledgement
INV-FOUND-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming warehouse operations baseline is reviewed. No governed write.
INV-FOUND-002.why: Operation baselines define the default receipt, delivery, and internal transfer behavior. Incorrect baselines mean every warehouse transaction follows the wrong process.
confirm_first: Has the default receipt process been reviewed (1-step, 2-step, 3-step)?
Has the default delivery process been reviewed?
Are internal transfer operations needed?
common_mistakes: Accepting default 1-step operations when the business requires quality checks or packing steps.

INV-FOUND-003.title: Operation types and sequences foundation
INV-FOUND-003.what: Establishes operation types and sequences on stock.warehouse (creates operation types when warehouse is configured). Defines how receipt, delivery, and internal operations are categorized.
INV-FOUND-003.why: Operation types control the picking flow, sequence numbering, and reporting categorization for all inventory movements.
confirm_first: Are the default operation types sufficient or are custom types needed?
Is the sequence numbering scheme acceptable?
Have operation type names been aligned with the client's warehouse terminology?
common_mistakes: Creating custom operation types when renaming defaults would suffice.
Not configuring return operation types, blocking customer and vendor returns.

INV-DREQ-001.title: Product-inventory structure alignment
INV-DREQ-001.what: Configures product-specific inventory settings (stock.picking.type/routes alignment) to ensure products move through the correct warehouse operations.
INV-DREQ-001.why: Products must be linked to the correct routes and operation types. Misalignment means products follow wrong warehouse flows.
confirm_first: Are products assigned to the correct warehouse routes?
Have storable products been distinguished from consumables?
Are lot/serial number tracking requirements defined per product?
common_mistakes: Not setting tracking (lot/serial) requirements before initial inventory is loaded.

INV-DREQ-002.title: Inventory valuation policy alignment
INV-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming inventory valuation settings align with accounting policy. Depends on ACCT-DREQ-003 when full accounting is active.
INV-DREQ-002.why: Inventory valuation generates accounting entries. If valuation and accounting are misaligned, the general ledger does not reflect actual inventory value.
confirm_first: Is inventory valuation set to manual or automated (perpetual)?
Has the costing method been confirmed (standard, AVCO, FIFO)?
Are product categories configured with the correct valuation accounts?
common_mistakes: Setting automated valuation without confirming the accounting accounts are mapped.
INV-DREQ-002.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Inventory Valuation

INV-DREQ-003.title: Multi-warehouse operation type configuration
INV-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (OP-02 >= 2). Configures operation types for multi-warehouse environments, including inter-warehouse transfer operations.
INV-DREQ-003.why: Multi-warehouse operations require transfer routes and operation types that correctly move stock between warehouses.
confirm_first: How do goods move between warehouses (direct transfer, transit location)?
Are inter-warehouse transfers tracked with separate operation types?
Has the transit time between warehouses been configured?
common_mistakes: Not creating inter-warehouse transfer routes, forcing manual stock adjustments.

INV-DREQ-004.title: 2-step receipt configuration
INV-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-03=2 steps). Configures 2-step receipt operations (receive + quality check / put-away) on stock.picking.type.
INV-DREQ-004.why: 2-step receipts add a quality or input step before goods reach stock. Without configuration, the extra step blocks goods from entering inventory.
confirm_first: What is the purpose of the second step (quality check, input zone)?
Are the input and stock locations correctly defined?
Has the 2-step flow been tested with a sample receipt?
common_mistakes: Enabling 2-step receipt without training warehouse staff on the additional step.

INV-DREQ-005.title: 3-step receipt configuration
INV-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-03=3 steps). Configures 3-step receipt operations (receive + quality + stock) on stock.picking.type.
INV-DREQ-005.why: 3-step receipts provide maximum control but add operational complexity. Each step must be correctly configured or goods get stuck in intermediate locations.
confirm_first: Does the business truly need 3 steps or would 2 suffice?
Are quality check criteria defined for the inspection step?
Has the full 3-step flow been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Implementing 3 steps when the business does not actually perform quality inspection on receipt.

INV-DREQ-006.title: Dropship inventory configuration
INV-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (PI-05=Yes). Informational confirmation that dropship routes are correctly configured in inventory. Depends on INV-FOUND-003.
INV-DREQ-006.why: Dropship routes bypass the warehouse entirely. Without correct configuration, dropship orders create phantom inventory movements.
confirm_first: Is the dropship route activated on the relevant products?
Are vendor locations correctly configured for dropship flows?
Has a dropship order been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Enabling dropship routes without configuring the partner/vendor delivery flow.

INV-DREQ-007.title: AVCO/FIFO valuation configuration
INV-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-02=AVCO or FIFO). Configures real-cost inventory valuation on stock.picking.type, linking to accounting valuation accounts.
INV-DREQ-007.why: AVCO and FIFO valuation generate automatic accounting entries on every stock movement. Misconfiguration causes incorrect cost of goods sold and inventory values.
confirm_first: Has the costing method decision (AVCO vs. FIFO) been confirmed by finance?
Are valuation accounts configured per product category (ACCT-DREQ-006)?
Has a test receipt been processed to verify valuation entries?
common_mistakes: Switching costing methods after initial inventory is loaded.
INV-DREQ-007.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Inventory Valuation

INV-DREQ-008.title: Manufacturing inventory integration
INV-DREQ-008.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-01=Yes). Informational confirmation that inventory is configured for manufacturing integration — component locations, finished goods locations, and production operation types.
INV-DREQ-008.why: Manufacturing consumes components from and produces finished goods to specific inventory locations. Misconfiguration causes production orders to fail stock moves.
confirm_first: Are component and finished goods locations defined?
Is the production operation type correctly configured?
Has a test manufacturing order been run to verify stock movements?
common_mistakes: Not configuring the production location, causing MO stock moves to use the wrong source.

INV-DREQ-009.title: Rental inventory integration
INV-DREQ-009.what: Conditional checkpoint (RM-01 includes 'Rental of assets or equipment'). Informational confirmation that inventory is configured for rental product tracking and return flow.
INV-DREQ-009.why: Rental products require specific inventory handling for checkout, return, and availability tracking. Without configuration, rental stock is not properly tracked.
confirm_first: Are rental products configured as storable products with serial tracking?
Is the rental return operation type configured?
Has the rental checkout and return flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not enabling serial tracking on rental products, losing individual asset traceability.

INV-GL-001.title: Inventory go-live readiness: operations
INV-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming warehouse operations are finalized and ready for production use.
INV-GL-001.why: Going live with inventory before routes, operation types, and warehouse structure are finalized creates operational chaos.
confirm_first: Has the warehouse team tested all major workflows (receipt, delivery, internal transfer)?
Are all routes and operation types finalized?
Has initial inventory been loaded or is a count planned?
common_mistakes: Going live without loading initial inventory balances.

INV-GL-002.title: Inventory go-live readiness: valuation
INV-GL-002.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming inventory valuation configuration is finalized and aligned with accounting.
INV-GL-002.why: Inventory valuation errors after go-live require complex corrections. Valuation must be right from the first transaction.
confirm_first: Has a test transaction verified that valuation entries post correctly?
Are opening inventory values loaded at the correct cost?
Has finance reviewed and approved the valuation setup?
common_mistakes: Loading opening inventory at zero cost, then trying to correct values after the fact.

manufacturing.label: Manufacturing (MRP)
manufacturing.overview: Governs production execution, bills of materials, work centers, and manufacturing control on mrp.workcenter. Manufacturing design depends on inventory and master data being correctly configured.

MRP-FOUND-001.title: Manufacturing module activation foundation
MRP-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the manufacturing module is activated and baseline settings are reviewed. No governed write.
MRP-FOUND-001.why: Manufacturing depends on inventory operation types being correctly configured first. Activating MRP without inventory foundations creates dependency conflicts.
confirm_first: Is the manufacturing module installed?
Has the production location been configured in the warehouse?
Are the basic manufacturing settings reviewed?
common_mistakes: Activating manufacturing before inventory warehouse and operation types are configured.

MRP-DREQ-001.title: BOM governance baseline
MRP-DREQ-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming BOM governance rules are defined — who can create/modify BOMs, versioning rules, and approval requirements.
MRP-DREQ-001.why: BOMs are the core data structure for manufacturing. Ungoverned BOM changes cause production errors, incorrect costing, and material shortages.
confirm_first: Who is authorized to create and modify BOMs?
Is there a BOM approval process?
Has a standard BOM template been defined for the business?
common_mistakes: Allowing all manufacturing users to modify BOMs without oversight.

MRP-DREQ-002.title: Work order and routing control
MRP-DREQ-002.what: Configures work center and routing definitions on mrp.workcenter, establishing the production process flow.
MRP-DREQ-002.why: Work centers define where production happens and how long it takes. Incorrect work center configuration produces wrong capacity planning and production scheduling.
confirm_first: What are the work centers in the production facility?
What is the capacity and working hours for each work center?
Have routing sequences been defined for the main products?
common_mistakes: Not setting work center capacity, causing scheduling to assume infinite capacity.
Creating one generic work center instead of modeling the actual production flow.

MRP-DREQ-003.title: Multi-level BOM configuration
MRP-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-02=Multi-level). Configures multi-level BOM structures on mrp.workcenter (routing for sub-assemblies).
MRP-DREQ-003.why: Multi-level BOMs create nested production orders for sub-assemblies. Without proper configuration, Odoo may not automatically plan sub-assembly production.
confirm_first: Has the BOM hierarchy been mapped (top-level to sub-assemblies)?
Are sub-assembly products configured as 'Manufacture' route?
Has a multi-level production order been tested?
common_mistakes: Not configuring replenishment rules for sub-assemblies, requiring manual MO creation.
MRP-DREQ-003.odoo_docs_hint: Manufacturing > Products > Bills of Materials > [BOM] > BOM Type

MRP-DREQ-004.title: Phantom BOM configuration
MRP-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-02=Phantom). Configures phantom/kit BOMs that explode into components without creating production orders.
MRP-DREQ-004.why: Phantom BOMs are kits — they decompose into components on sales orders or production orders. Incorrect configuration means components are not consumed correctly.
confirm_first: Which products are kits that should use phantom BOMs?
Are kit components configured with correct product types?
Has a sales order with a phantom BOM product been tested?
common_mistakes: Using phantom BOMs for products that actually need production orders.
MRP-DREQ-004.odoo_docs_hint: Manufacturing > Products > Bills of Materials > [BOM] > BOM Type = Kit

MRP-DREQ-005.title: Work center capacity and scheduling
MRP-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-03=Yes). Informational confirmation that work center capacity and scheduling parameters are configured.
MRP-DREQ-005.why: Without capacity configuration, production scheduling assumes work centers have unlimited capacity, creating unrealistic production plans.
confirm_first: Are working hours configured for each work center?
Is the time efficiency (OEE) set correctly?
Has production scheduling been tested with realistic order volumes?
common_mistakes: Leaving default capacity values, producing schedules that do not match reality.
MRP-DREQ-005.odoo_docs_hint: Manufacturing > Configuration > Work Centers > [Work Center] > General Information

MRP-DREQ-006.title: Work center costing configuration
MRP-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-03=Yes). Informational confirmation that work center costing rates are configured for production cost calculation.
MRP-DREQ-006.why: Work center costs feed into product costing through routings. Incorrect rates mean production cost calculations are wrong.
confirm_first: Has the cost per hour been set for each work center?
Does the costing rate include overhead or just direct labor?
Has finance reviewed the work center costing rates?
common_mistakes: Leaving work center cost at zero, causing production to show no labor cost.
MRP-DREQ-006.odoo_docs_hint: Manufacturing > Configuration > Work Centers > [Work Center] > Costing

MRP-DREQ-007.title: Subcontracting configuration
MRP-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-04=Yes). Informational confirmation that subcontracting is configured — subcontractor locations, BOM subcontracting type, and component shipping.
MRP-DREQ-007.why: Subcontracting automates component shipment to subcontractors and receipt of finished goods. Misconfiguration breaks the entire subcontracting flow.
confirm_first: Are subcontractor partners configured with subcontracting locations?
Are BOMs set to 'Subcontracting' type?
Has a subcontracting order been tested end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Not configuring subcontractor locations, causing component shipments to fail.

MRP-DREQ-008.title: Manufacturing-accounting cost integration
MRP-DREQ-008.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-01=Full accounting AND MF-02=Multi-level). Configures manufacturing cost accounting integration for multi-level production.
MRP-DREQ-008.why: Multi-level manufacturing with full accounting generates complex cost flows through WIP accounts. Without proper configuration, production costs are misallocated.
confirm_first: Are WIP (work-in-progress) accounts configured?
Has the cost flow through multi-level production been mapped?
Has finance verified cost entries from a test production run?
common_mistakes: Not mapping WIP accounts, causing production cost entries to fail.
MRP-DREQ-008.odoo_docs_hint: Inventory > Configuration > Product Categories > Account Properties > Stock Accounts

MRP-GL-001.title: Manufacturing go-live readiness: production
MRP-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming manufacturing configuration is finalized for production use.
MRP-GL-001.why: Manufacturing errors after go-live affect inventory accuracy, production scheduling, and cost reporting.
confirm_first: Has the production team tested the full MO lifecycle?
Are all BOMs, work centers, and routings finalized?
Has the production team been trained?
common_mistakes: Going live without testing a complete manufacturing order cycle.

MRP-GL-002.title: Manufacturing go-live readiness: inventory-accounting alignment
MRP-GL-002.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming manufacturing's inventory and accounting integration is finalized.
MRP-GL-002.why: Manufacturing touches inventory (component consumption, finished goods) and accounting (cost entries). Both must be aligned before go-live.
confirm_first: Has a production order been run and verified against inventory and accounting?
Are cost variance accounts configured if standard costing is used?
Has finance signed off on the manufacturing cost flow?
common_mistakes: Going live with manufacturing without verifying that production cost entries are correct.

plm.label: PLM
plm.overview: Governs engineering change control (ECO) linked to manufacturing structures. PLM depends on manufacturing BOM governance being established. Coverage gap: mrp.eco and mrp.eco.type are not in the governed apply surface.

PLM-FOUND-001.title: Engineering change control foundation
PLM-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the PLM foundation — confirms engineering change orders (ECO) are available and BOM revision governance is understood.
PLM-FOUND-001.why: ECOs are the formal mechanism for BOM changes in regulated or controlled manufacturing. Without PLM foundation, BOM changes are untracked.
confirm_first: Is the PLM module installed?
Has the engineering team been introduced to the ECO workflow?
Are ECO stage definitions aligned with the engineering change process?
common_mistakes: Installing PLM without a clear process for when ECOs are required vs. direct BOM edits.

PLM-DREQ-001.title: ECO approval design
PLM-DREQ-001.what: Configures ECO approval workflows — who can create, review, and approve engineering changes. Depends on USR-DREQ-010 (PLM access configuration).
PLM-DREQ-001.why: Without an approval design, engineering changes either cannot be approved or are approved by unauthorized users.
confirm_first: Who are the ECO approvers?
Is there a multi-stage approval process (review, test, approve)?
Has the approval workflow been tested with a sample ECO?
common_mistakes: Not assigning ECO approvers, blocking all engineering changes.

PLM-DREQ-002.title: ECO-BOM revision governance
PLM-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the relationship between ECOs and BOM revisions is understood and documented. Coverage gap: mrp.eco.type not in governed apply surface.
PLM-DREQ-002.why: ECOs that create BOM revisions must follow a controlled process. Without governance, BOM versions proliferate without traceability.
confirm_first: How are BOM versions tracked (ECO-driven vs. manual)?
Is there a naming convention for BOM revisions?
Who decides when a BOM revision is released to production?
common_mistakes: Creating BOM revisions outside the ECO process, losing change traceability.
PLM-DREQ-002.odoo_docs_hint: PLM > Engineering Change Orders > [ECO] > BOM Changes

PLM-REC-001.title: PLM documentation linkage
PLM-REC-001.what: Recommended checkpoint for linking engineering documents to ECOs and BOM revisions.
PLM-REC-001.why: Engineering changes without supporting documentation (drawings, specifications) are difficult to verify and audit.
confirm_first: Will engineering drawings be attached to ECOs?
Is there a document management process for engineering specifications?
Has the Documents module been configured for PLM use?
common_mistakes: Approving ECOs without attaching supporting engineering documentation.

hr.label: HR
hr.overview: Governs employee records, department structure, and HR-related operational policies on hr.department, hr.job, and res.company. HR structure affects approval workflows across multiple domains.

HR-FOUND-001.title: Employee and department structure foundation
HR-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the HR department structure on hr.department, defining organizational units and their hierarchy.
HR-FOUND-001.why: Department structure drives approval chains, reporting lines, and cost center allocation. Every domain that uses manager approval depends on HR structure.
confirm_first: What is the organizational department structure?
Has the department hierarchy been mapped to the Odoo department model?
Who are the department managers?
common_mistakes: Creating a flat department structure when the business needs hierarchical approval chains.
Not assigning department managers, breaking approval workflows.

HR-DREQ-001.title: Employee record governance
HR-DREQ-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming employee record governance rules are defined — who creates employee records, required fields, and data privacy.
HR-DREQ-001.why: Employee records contain sensitive data. Without governance, records may be incomplete, inaccurate, or inappropriately accessed.
confirm_first: Who is responsible for creating and maintaining employee records?
What fields are mandatory for the business (emergency contact, contract type)?
Are there data privacy requirements for employee information?
common_mistakes: Creating employee records with minimal data, then needing to backfill critical fields later.

HR-DREQ-002.title: Job position structure
HR-DREQ-002.what: Configures job positions on hr.job, defining the role taxonomy within the organization.
HR-DREQ-002.why: Job positions drive recruitment, reporting, and organizational clarity. Without defined positions, employee records lack role context.
confirm_first: Has the client provided a list of job positions?
Are positions mapped to departments?
Will job positions be used for recruitment?
common_mistakes: Creating too many granular job positions that add maintenance overhead without value.

HR-DREQ-003.title: HR leave management configuration
HR-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes HR leave). Configures leave types and allocation rules on res.company HR settings.
HR-DREQ-003.why: Leave management affects employee availability, project planning, and payroll. Incorrect configuration causes leave balance errors.
confirm_first: What leave types does the business offer (annual, sick, unpaid)?
Are leave allocations annual or accrual-based?
Who approves leave requests?
common_mistakes: Not configuring leave allocations, causing employees to have zero leave balance.

HR-DREQ-004.title: Timesheet configuration
HR-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (RM-02=Yes). Informational confirmation that timesheet configuration is aligned with project and billing requirements.
HR-DREQ-004.why: Timesheets feed into project billing and employee productivity reporting. Misconfiguration means unbillable hours or incorrect invoicing.
confirm_first: Which employees will use timesheets?
Will timesheets be used for client billing?
Has the timesheet approval workflow been defined?
common_mistakes: Enabling timesheets without training employees on how to use them.

pos.label: POS
pos.overview: Governs point-of-sale session control, cashier access, payment methods, and accounting linkage on pos.payment.method. POS configuration depends on foundation settings and accounting being correct. Coverage gap: pos.config not in governed apply surface.

POS-FOUND-001.title: POS module activation foundation
POS-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming POS module is activated and payment method baseline (pos.payment.method) is reviewed. No governed write.
POS-FOUND-001.why: POS depends on payment methods being correctly configured before any sessions can operate. Missing payment methods block POS operations.
confirm_first: Is the POS module installed?
Have the default payment methods been reviewed (cash, card, etc.)?
Has the POS shop/config been created?
common_mistakes: Opening POS sessions before configuring payment methods.
POS-FOUND-001.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Payment Methods

POS-DREQ-001.title: POS session control configuration
POS-DREQ-001.what: Configures POS session control rules on pos.payment.method, defining session opening/closing procedures and cash control.
POS-DREQ-001.why: Session control determines how cash is managed, when sessions must be closed, and what reconciliation is required. Without controls, cash discrepancies go undetected.
confirm_first: Is opening cash control required (counting cash at session start)?
Is closing cash control required (counting cash at session end)?
What is the maximum session duration allowed?
common_mistakes: Disabling cash control to speed up operations, losing visibility into cash discrepancies.
POS-DREQ-001.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Point of Sale > [POS] > Payment

POS-DREQ-002.title: POS product and pricing configuration
POS-DREQ-002.what: Configures POS-specific product availability and pricing on pos.payment.method (linked config).
POS-DREQ-002.why: POS product availability determines what items appear on the POS interface. Incorrect configuration means products are missing or wrong prices are shown.
